name: TJX Companies

labor.s: 4 OSHA severe-injury reports in past 2y (0 amputations); 16 since 2015.

guns.s: No federal firearms license on record for this company in the ATF FFL registry — no licensed firearms dealing or manufacturing.

privacy.s: 1 publicly-disclosed data breach per Have I Been Pwned (2007) — 94M accounts compromised. Most recent: TJX Companies (2007).

execPay.s: CEO-to-median-worker pay ratio 774:1 (median employee pay $14,994) — SEC proxy statement (DEF 14A, 2026).

seedNote: TJ Maxx, Marshalls, HomeGoods, Sierra, HomeSense

health.s: 14 CPSC recalls on record; sample hazard: the wooden frame of the chair can break or collapse when a consumer is seated in.

governance.s: Wikidata: owned by S&P 500; HQ Framingham. (Wikidata Q1285819)

sampleRecords.event_description: Fall on same level due to slip or trip
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee went to lift a carton and tripped on a different carton that was on the floor. She fell on the floor in the backroom and sustained a fractured right hip, and abrasions to her arm and knee. The employee was hospitalized and required surgery.

Two employees were performing merchandising duties and team lifting a wicker-style end table. The injured employee lost their grip on the table and it fell. The employee then lost their balance and fell to the floor, resulting in a fractured right leg near the knee.
An employee was using a manual pallet jack when they lost their balance and fell on the concrete floor. The employee sustained a fractured right hip.

sampleRecords.nature: Cuts, lacerations, punctures without injury to internal structures
sampleRecords.event_description: Fall on same level due to slip or trip
sampleRecords.final_narrative: On September 5, 2024, at around 6:04 PM, an employee was leaving work for the day, tripped over the foot of a TV stand in the breakroom, and hit their head on a table. The employee was hospitalized with a laceration to the right side of their forehead.

sampleRecords.event_description: Fall on same level due to tripping over an object
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was walking to get a box of supplies when she tripped over a product box that was on the floor. She fell and fractured her left femur.

topHazards.label: The wooden frame of the chair can break or collapse when a consumer is seated in the chair, posing a fall hazard.
The recalled bookcases are unstable and can tip over if not anchored to the wall, posing tip-over and entrapment hazards that can result in death or injuries t…
The chairs can tip over or collapse when a consumer is seated in the chair, posing a fall hazard.
The resin and medium-density fiberboard ("MDF") menorahs can burn or catch fire when holding lit candles, posing a fire hazard.
The counter stools can break or collapse, posing fall and injury hazards.

sampleRecalls.title: TJX Recalls Foldable Bistro Set Chairs Due to Fall Hazard; Sold at Marshalls, T.J. Maxx, HomeGoods and Homesense Stores
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves wooden folding chairs sold as part of three-piece foldable bistro sets under the Panama Jack and House & Garden brands. The sets consist of one small wooden table and two foldable chairs made with wood frames and black, anthracite, navy blue, beige, olive green, or off-white roping on the seat and…
hazards: The wooden frame of the chair can break or collapse when a consumer is seated in the chair, posing a fall hazard.
injuries: The firm has received seven reports of the wooden frame of the chair breaking or collapsing when a consumer was seated on the chair, including four reports of minor injuries.

sampleRecalls.title: TJX Recalls Bookcases Due to Tip-Over and Entrapment Hazards; Imported by Furniture Source International; Sold at HomeGoods
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves all Aderes bookcases. The bell-shaped bookcases have a natural finish, measure 44" x 18" x 86" and have two glass cabinet doors and six drawers. The bookcase sits on top of four legs that are affixed to two boards that are screwed into the bottom of the bookcase with eight screws on each side. The…
hazards: The recalled bookcases are unstable and can tip over if not anchored to the wall, posing tip-over and entrapment hazards that can result in death or injuries t…
injuries: TJX has received two reports of bookcases that were unstable and began to tip forward. No injuries have been reported.

sampleRecalls.title: TJX Recalls Egg Chairs Due to Fall Hazard; Sold at Marshalls, T.J. Maxx, HomeGoods and Homesense Stores
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves nest swing egg chairs sold under the Tommy Bahama and Martha Stewart brands. The chairs have a metal circle base that attaches to a metal pole, from which the oval-shaped wicker chairs hang by hook and chain. The chairs were sold with a round cushion for seating and a hangtag with the brand name "…
hazards: The chairs can tip over or collapse when a consumer is seated in the chair, posing a fall hazard.
injuries: The firm has received 27 reports of the chairs collapsing or tipping over, including 19 reports of injuries, including cuts, scrapes, soreness and one report of broken ribs and a collapsed lung.

sampleRecalls.title: TJX Recalls Menorahs Due to Fire Hazard; Sold at Marshalls, HomeGoods and Homesense Stores
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves resin and medium-density fiberboard ("MDF") menorahs with inserts for nine candles. The menorahs were sold in the following shapes: llama, brontosaurus, stegosaurus, T-rex, castle, rainbow, rainbow platform and emojis. The menorahs were sold with a white hangtag with an image of a blue and silver …
hazards: The resin and medium-density fiberboard ("MDF") menorahs can burn or catch fire when holding lit candles, posing a fire hazard.
injuries: TJX has received three reports in the U.S. of the menorahs burning or catching on fire when holding lit candles. No injuries or property damage have been reported.

sampleRecalls.title: TJX Recalls Counter Stools Due to Fall and Injury Hazards; Sold at HomeGoods and Homesense Stores
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves swivel counter stools with wooden legs and fabric backrests with rivet trim around the seat. The stools were sold in gray faux leather, and light gray, dark gray and blue/cream herringbone fabrics. The stools measure approximately 36 inches to the top of the backrest, and the seat height is approx…
hazards: The counter stools can break or collapse, posing fall and injury hazards.
injuries: TJX has received 20 reports of stools breaking in which consumers fell, including 17 reports of minor injuries.
